<a id="t-42fe66f55e"></a>
## Thread: Unix, COBOL, CGI Interface — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Web, XML, modern integration`](topics.md#web)_

### Unix, COBOL, CGI Interface

- **From:** dons06@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8ngn9o$lk7$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
MF COBOL is our industrial strength language running on HP-UX.  The
Micro Focus COBOL predates Server Express and works well. PowerBuilder
is currently our client side GUI.  We want to switch from PowerBuilder
GUIs to a browser interface.  We intend to use Apache as the server (on
UNIX).  MS Frontpage is the likely editor.

We need an interface that merges our data with the editor generated
HTML, then interfaces this to Apache.

Several newer COBOLs, such as Fujitsu and Server Express accomplish the
above but with major acquisition costs and some operational downsides.
Ideally, someone can suggest/recommend appropriate middleware that will
meet our objective.

     All help appreciated.  Thanks.
                Don


Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

#### ↳ Re: Unix, COBOL, CGI Interface

- **From:** g_t_g@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nhdum$hve$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<8ngn9o$lk7$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Check out COBOL CGIX from England Technical Services at:
showcase.netins.net/web/etsinc/

This may be exactly what you are looking for.


Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

#### ↳ Re: Unix, COBOL, CGI Interface

- **From:** "Gazaloo" <gaz@home.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<vlTm5.113889$i5.1650220@news1.frmt1.sfba.home.com>`
- **References:** `<8ngn9o$lk7$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
<dons06@my-deja.com> wrote:
> Several newer COBOLs, such as Fujitsu and Server Express accomplish the
> above but with major acquisition costs and some operational downsides.
> Ideally, someone can suggest/recommend appropriate middleware that will
> meet our objective.

what version of Micro Focus COBOL are you using? if it's Object COBOL
Developer Suite V4.1.30, then i'm pretty certain that this will handle the
syntax extensions (ACCEPT/DISPLAY & EXEC HTML) necessary for CGI
programming.
```

## Thread: Import Library Newbie — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### Import Library Newbie

- **From:** "Grinder" <no.spam@no.spam.spam.spam.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Tu%m5.34486$B86.1031827@nntp2.onemain.com>`

```
Can someone offer me some assistance in linking a MF Visual Cobol program to
an existing Win32 DLL?

I'll provide more details as necessary, but basically I want to do this:

1)  Create a stdcall DLL in Delphi 5.
2)  Link that DLL to a Visual COBOL program.

Step #1 is accomplished, but I'm having difficulty with #2.  It looks as if
my shortfall is in creating the proper LIB file.
```

#### ↳ Re: Import Library Newbie

- **From:** "Cheesle" <cheesle@online.NoSpamPlease.no>
- **Date:** 2000-08-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8njo9l$rsj$1@news.cerf.net>`
- **References:** `<Tu%m5.34486$B86.1031827@nntp2.onemain.com>`

```
"Grinder" <no.spam@no.spam.spam.spam.net> wrote in message
news:Tu%m5.34486$B86.1031827@nntp2.onemain.com...
> Step #1 is accomplished, but I'm having difficulty with #2.  It looks as
if
> my shortfall is in creating the proper LIB file.

I don't know Visual Cobol, but you should not need the LIB file. Load the
DLL dynamically and use GetProcAddress to assign the procedure pointer. Be
aware of the differences of passing parameters by value and by address
(common programmer error).

Cheesle
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: MF cobol api call with structure containing array

- **From:** tracey@bframe.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8njimu$v27$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<8njdqm$ov9$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <8njg08$c1c$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
MQSendMessage

HRESULT APIENTRY MQSendMessage (
  QUEUEHANDLE hDestinationQueue,
  MQMSGPROPS *pMessageProps,
  ITransaction *pTransaction
);

Prior to trying to send the message, I locate the handle to the queue
using a function, and open the queue as send.  I can also close the
queue.  I believe I've got the first and last parameter right.
MQMSGPROPS is my problem.  I'm tring to be very simple to get this
thing started, just send the body of the message only.

MQMSGPROPS
typedef struct tagMQMSGPROPS
{
  DWORD cProp;
  MSGPROPID *aPropID;
  MQPROPVARIANT *PropVar;
  HRESULT *aSTatus;
}  MQMSGPROPS;

cProp   - Number of properites in the set
        - A count indicating how many properties are supplied.
aPropID - Property identifiers
        - An array of PROPID values identifying which properties
          are spected for the call.
aPropVar - Value of properties in the set
         - An array of MQPROPVARIANT structures containing the values
           of the properties.
aStatus  - Optional.  Returns errors that indicate the status
           of the properties in the set.
         - An array of HRESULT values returned by MSMQ.


I believe MQPROPVARIANT is the root of the problem.

MQPROPVARIANT

struct tagMQPROPVARIANT {
  VARTYPE vt;
  WORD wReserved1;
  WORD wReserved2;
  WORD wReserved3;
  union {
    UCHAR bVal;     /* VT_UI1  */
    short iVal;     /* VT_I2   */
    ... there's more - the next is the one I must use to send 'body'
    LPWSTR pwszVal; /* VT_VECTOR|VT_UI1  */
    };
};
typedef struct MWPROPVARIANT MQPROPVARIANT;

vt - The type indicator of the property.
wReserved1,2,3 - Reserved by MSMQ
union - Specifies the vlaue of the property.


Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: MF cobol api call with structure containing array

- **From:** Stephen.Biggs@merant.com (Steve Biggs)
- **Date:** 2000-08-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8njrfd$ua2$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<8njdqm$ov9$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <8njg08$c1c$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net> <8njimu$v27$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
tracey@bframe.com wrote:
>MQMSGPROPS
>typedef struct tagMQMSGPROPS
…[23 more quoted lines elided]…
>typedef struct MWPROPVARIANT MQPROPVARIANT;

This is very similar (identical, so far as I can see) to a COM (OLE) 
VARIANT structure.

Net Express ships with two copybooks called "mfole.cpy" and 
"vartypes.cpy". The former contains the possible VT- types and the base 
VARIANT typedefs (VARIANT and VARIANTARG) which reuse the (re)definitions 
in the latter. 

You could either use mfole.cpy/VARIANTs directly, or do something similar 
for clarity, eg:

copy "mfole.cpy".
01 PROPVARIANT typedef.
  03 PROPVARIANT-vt VARTYPE.
  03 PROPVARIANT-reserved1  pic xx comp-5.
  03 PROPVARIANT-reserved2  pic xx comp-5.
  03 PROPVARIANT-reserved3  pic xx comp-5.
  copy "vartypes.cpy" replacing ==()== by ==PROPVARIANT==.
..
(If you're not using Net Express, vartypes contains lots of redefinitions, 
such as:

         03 ()-data      PIC xxxxxxxx.
         03 ()-VT-UI1        REDEFINES ()-data PIC x COMP-5.
         03 ()-VT-I1         REDEFINES ()-data PIC s9(2) COMP-5.
         03 ()-VT-I2         REDEFINES ()-data PIC s9(4) COMP-5.
         03 ()-VT-UI2        REDEFINES ()-data PIC xx COMP-5.
One for each type}
..
01 MQMSGPROPS typedef.
  01 cProp  pic x(4) comp-5.
  01 aPropIdPtr  pointer. 
  01 aPropVarPtr  pointer. 
  01 aStatusPtr  pointer. 
..
01 myMessageProps MQMSGPROPS.
01 myPropVariants PROPVARIANT OCCURS 5.
..
set aPropVarPtr of myMessageProps to address of myPropVariants(1)

Note that you need to use pointers for C arrays, and set the value of 
those pointers to the addresses of the arrays themselves.

(Alternatively, you could follow the MSMQOLE demo shipped with Net 
Express, which demonstrates sending to MSMQ using OLE automation rather 
than MQ API and C structures. :-)

Hope that helps.

Steve.
```

#### ↳ Re: MF cobol api call with structure containing array

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8njv5u$686$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<8njdqm$ov9$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
I know that you have posted the C code and that HOPEFULLY someone with good C
and MF COBOL ability will provide you the specific information to meet your
needs.  HOWEVER, I can show you (in the abstract), how I would do this in
COBOL (using Micro Focus extensions - but not all of them).

Linkage Section.
01 Passed-Parm.
    05    Addr1    Pointer.
    05    Addr2    Pointer.
    05    Addr3    Pointer.
01    FirstString Pic X(99) *> or whatever the string length is
01    2ndString  Pic X(99)
01    Structure1.
    05    SubString1    Pic X.
    05    SubString2    Pic X.
    05    SubAddr        Pointer.
01    AnotherString    Pic X.
Procedure Division Using Passed-Parm. *> do not put other 01's here
  ....
Set Address of FirstString to Addr1
Set Address of 2ndString to Addr2
Set Address of Structure1 to Addr3.
Set Address of AnotherString to SubAddr.

   ****

Does this make the "technique" clear.  (If you are getting "null-terminated
strings" - you will need to deal with those too, but I didn't think that was
what you were asking about.)
```

want their product out in the public domain. Not that anything is all that

#### ↳ R: Cobol and compilation

- **From:** "Pasquale Fusco" <pafusc@tin.it>
- **Date:** 2000-08-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nllhf$8ea$1@nslave2.tin.it>`
- **References:** `<qgn8ztuavq0.fsf@sheeana.i-did-not-set--mail-host-address--so-shoot-me>`

```
cedric baudoin <baudoi_c@epita.fr
> wrote in message
qgn8ztuavq0.fsf@sheeana.i-did-not-set--mail-host-address--so-shoot-me...
>
> Student in a french computer sciences school, I'm looking
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Italiano - - - - - - -
Ci provo io.
I compilatori servono a tradurre le istruzioni scritte
con un Linguaggio di programmazione, in un Linguaggio Macchina,
che � l'unico insieme di istruzioni comprensibili dal computer.

Per esempio, l'istruzione Cobol "ADD 1 TO FIELDX", per essere
capito ed eseguito dal computer, deve essere tradotto (=compilato)
nella istruzione macchina "FA02120034" (ho messo dei numeri a caso).

Ogni linguaggio di programmazione ha il proprio compilatore,
con l'unico scopo di traduzione in linguaggio macchina.

Il risultato della compilazione di un programma
viene raccolto in un file (programma oggetto), il quale
per essere veramente eseguibile, dovra' essere assemblato
con altri elementi del compilatore (librerie), per mezzo
di un programma che si chiama Linker.

Vantaggi del Cobol: molto facile da imparare; molto diffuso.
Svantaggi: le versioni pi� complete e moderne sono
molto costose (questa � solo una mia opinione).
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - [Almost] English - - - - - -
Here I prove.
The compilers serve to translate the written instructions
with an linguage of programming, in an Computer-linguage,
that it is the unique together of apprehensible instructions from the
computer.

For example, the Cobol instruction "ADD 1 TO FIELDX", for be
understood and performed from the computer, it must be translated
(=compiled)
in the computer-instruction "FA02120034" (I have put of the numbers at
random).

Each linguage of programming has the own compiler,
with the only purpose of translation in Computer-linguage.

The result of the compilation of a program
comes collected in a file (program object), which
for be really executable, it will be assembled
with other elements of the compiler (libraries), for means
of a program that is called Linker.

Advantages of Cobol: very easy from learn; very diffuse.
Disadvantages: the more complete and modern releases are
very expensive (this is only a my opinion).
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
Ciao.
Pasquale Fusco
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol and compilation

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<deun5.22$gM4.15958@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net>`
- **References:** `<qgn8ztuavq0.fsf@sheeana.i-did-not-set--mail-host-address--so-shoot-me>`

```
All two-pass compilers work pretty much the same way. COBOL is no exception:

1. Scan source. Parse lines into programmer-created symbols - size and type
for datanames, library module(s) and any needed symbols for them for
language (COBOL, C++, whatever) verbs. On error, report. Retain symbol
table.

2. Scan again, converting symbols to their offsets for calls or moves. Where
necessary, insert calls to cast (convert) data to correct symbol types.

3. If no error, link generated modules with library modules, add import
tables, resolve cross-module references.

Is that what you meant? Or was there something more specific you were
interested in?
```

I am new for Cobol and want to find a Cobol Compiler on Window 98/95.
I have "Fujitsu on Windows 95/NT" come with the book "Cobol for
Dummies", but I am getting error message when trying to link OBJ file:
"
/DEBUG /DEBUGTYPE:COFF
/OUT:C:\FSC\PCOBOL32\SAMPLES\SAMPLE2\SAMPLE2.EXE
C:\FSC\PCOBOL32\samples\SAMPLE2\Sample2.OBJ
C:\FSC\PCOBOL32\PROJECT.RES

C:\FSC\PCOBOL32\LIBC.LIB

Linking files ended.
Please close the window."

it is not the problem from the code, but come from Fujitsu.

I am thinking to buy "Microfocus Personal Cobol for Windows", but could
not find it in local book store. Any advise? As a beginner, should I buy
it or just need to fix the problem on Configuration Fujitsu Compiler?

Parker

define one of your programs as 'main'. The GUI has an option to
do this - it is an option when you compile the module concerned.

Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month

Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Sunday, July 2, 2000

The COBOL FAQ has moved.  To view the most recent version, please check out the
web version at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send private
email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

The following is Appendix B.5 - Changes to create Version 3.01

 o Recorded new location of the FAQ
 o Updated some old (bad) web links
 o Added some new COBOL books (particularly those written or contributed to - by
frequent comp.lang.cobol submitters).
 o Added information about LegacyJ (PERCobol) and changed the company name from
"Synkronix" to "LegacyJ".
 o Updated information for changes in product and company names and addresses
where available
 o Updated the URLs for downloading the draft of the next COBOL Standard and
CobCy
 o Started the Samples/Example Section
 o Tried to get rid of most specific references to Windows/NT, Windows/95,
Windows/98, and Windows/2000.
 o Added links to Xinotech
 o Added information on Norcom and GUI ScreenIO
 o Added (back) information on Wang
 o Added (back) information on the Tiny-COBOL ('74 Standard) project

Eucalypt Software - Makers of Autopost & Rocketfuel --- Ripped Me Off!!!
-
All potential buyers should be warned to steer clear of this company. They have a repeated pattern
of ripping people off and ignoring requests for support.
-
I know someone who  sent in the $10 registration fee for Autopost TWICE (thinking the first one may
have gotten lost) and in both instances, never got a registered version of the program and all of his
emails were completely ignored.
-
I myself purchased Rocketfuel on-line and had to pull teeth to get an unlock code. The web site was very
quick to take my money, but no registration/unlock code came for days and days. I repeatedly emailed them,
only to receive nothing but autoresponses. After 5 days I sent them a message stating that if I did not have
a working version of the program by the close of business that day, that I would challenge the credit card
charge. Magically, the code arrived several hours later. 
-
What kind of way to do business is that? 
-
In any case, its academic... there are other products available for free that do virtually the same thing
and I only wish that I had known about them before I wasted my time with Eucalypt Software.
-
If your in need of automatic news posting software, there is nothing better than "NNTP Power Post 2000".
It's graciously been made available free from the following web site:
-
http://net-toys.8k.com/
-
Save your money... your time... and the aggravation!
-
Peace,
-
PPN
-
-
PS: I have no affiliation with the makers of PowerPost 2000.
-

-
-

## Thread: Compiling Cobol650 — 1 msgs

### Re: Compiling Cobol650

- **From:** SkippyPB <swiegand@neo.rr.com.nospam>
- **Date:** 2000-08-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<EA32B8AEA7914736.DF4FCEFAB2B98ED0.85190B7993734F79@lp.airnews.net>`

```
To all who are interested, I have some good news and some bad news.

The good news:  I cranked up my old Samsung 286 laptop which is purely
a DOS machine and has had a dead CMOS battery for over 3 years.  But
with loving care and a re-entry of all CMOS parameters, it works.
There I found PCTOOLS v6.  I used it to restore from floppy to an
IOMEGA Zip disk my backed up version of Cobol.  Now I had a Cobol.zip
file which I loaded into my Win 98 system and with the magic provided
by WinZip was able to take a peek in the zip file.

Now the bad news.  The Cobol is NOT Cobol650.  Rather, it is MS-COBOL
v5.  Sorry about that.  Again I have proven that Ginko Biloba does
nothing for ones memory.

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

What do people in China call their good dishes?







Boycott Mitsubishi Industries
For more info, please see:  
http://www.ran.org/ran/ran_campaigns/mitsubishi/background.html

Remove nospam to email me.

 Steve
```

## Thread: Announce: TinyCOBOL release (0.1.5.3) — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Open-source COBOL (GnuCOBOL, OpenCOBOL, TinyCOBOL)`](topics.md#open-source)_

### Announce: TinyCOBOL release (0.1.5.3)

- **From:** David Essex <dessex@arvotek.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,comp.compilers
- **Message-ID:** `<00-08-097@comp.compilers>`

```
A new version (0.1.5.3) of the Tiny COBOL compiler is available for
download at the Tiny COBOL home page (1) and at Metalab (2).

In this release, along with some bug fixes, the INITIALIZE, CURRENCY,
PERFORM WITH TEST BEFORE/AFTER option statements have been added to the
TC COBOL grammar.

Please forward any comments, suggestions to the TC mailing list (3).


David

1) Tiny COBOL home page
   http://tiny-cobol.sourceforge.net/
2) Metalab
   http://metalab.unc.edu/pub/Linux/devel/lang/cobol/
3) Tiny COBOL mailing list
   tiny-cobol-users@sourceforge.net
```

## Thread: statistics — 4 this month / 6 total

### statistics

- **From:** "No���l Garnier" <noel.garnier@abp.nl>
- **Date:** 2000-08-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nr4fg$1cn17@abp.nl>`

```
Hello,

we are trying to estimate the time batch programs of ours will run.
Therefore we need some statistics concerning the following actions.

- Opening  a sequential file
- closing a sequential file
- reading a sequential file
- writing to a  sequential file

we are using cobol for os/390 with language environment

Thanks in advance.

No�l Garnier
ABP Heerlen
```

#### ↳ Re: statistics

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nr7re$f1d$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<8nr4fg$1cn17@abp.nl>`

```
I am afraid that this is one of those "unanswerable" questions.  It certainly
will depend upon your workload, hardware, etc.  You mention "how long it will
run" - I assume this means that you are interested in "clock time" and not
CPU time.

My personal suggestion would depend upon whether your file layouts (or actual
files) already exist or not.

If the files exist (or can be created in a test version), then I would
suggest that you write a "dummy program" that simply  opens all the files
then loops thru reads/writes of the appropriate files, and closes them.  Then
run this job (not impacting any product files OF COURSE) at the same time
that you plan on running your final program (and on a machine with its normal
work load) -
   and see what happens.
```

#### ↳ Re: statistics

- **From:** Ken Foskey <waratah@zip.com.au>
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39A26A96.ADD934C7@zip.com.au>`
- **References:** `<8nr4fg$1cn17@abp.nl>`

```
"Noï¿½l Garnier" wrote:
> 
> - Opening  a sequential file
> - closing a sequential file
> - reading a sequential file
> - writing to a  sequential file

These are not easily answered,  as Bill said, the only real answer is
a long time...

If you are really concerned with IO take the following simple rules:

a)  Do not open and close datasets, it is very expensive.

b)  If your output is sequential and you use a lot of it, use BUFNO. 
I use BUFNO =50 to good effect on some of my runs.

c)  If you are using VSAM clusters look up Batch LSR's,  on bad code I
reduced run time from 60+ minutes to 10.  One reasonable code I got
about a 30% reduction.

If you want to really track this I can recommend a product called
STROBE.  It is pretty pricey, if you need to make it sing then it is
your tool.

If you have simple monitoring requirements I have a program that works
over SMF dumps and pulls some basic information, basically for
charging algorithms. SMF can give you step timings for example. It is
surprising when user pays how many tune ups happen to code.

Thanks
Ken Foskey
http://www.zipworld.com.au/~waratah/

For fast secure document delivery on the Net
http://www.themailxchange.com.au/
```

#### ↳ Re: statistics

- **From:** Susan Allen <susan.a.allen@boeing.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39A42CCC.142E070B@boeing.com>`
- **References:** `<8nr4fg$1cn17@abp.nl>`

```
"Noï¿½l Garnier" wrote:
> 
> Hello,
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

the reading and the writing of the sequential file is the real unknown
-- how big is each record?, how much do you process it?, how many
records are you processing?

I have a job that runs 12 hours (reading 16 million 32K records, each
record can write anywhere from 5 records (the minimum) to around 3,000
records, the job continues on to load some DB2 tables with the results
of the program output (those steps add to the wall time).

I have had other jobs that ran an hour or less, reading 16 to 18 million
records, the real time is the number of tape mounts, but it has smaller
records which process very quickly.

	Susan
```

## Thread: meaning of file status 39 — 8 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### meaning of file status 39

- **From:** baweber313@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nroua$rl2$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Hello All,

The file that I need to "read", as if it were a flat file, is an
indexed sequential file that was compressed in the COBOL program
creating the file by using $set datacompress "1" immediately prior to
the select statement.  The program is compiled using a MicroFocus COBOL
compiler.

I have never used MicroFocus COBOL before, as my prior experience is
all mainframe COBOL.  I have no idea how to use the MicroFocus
utilities, and there is no documentation at this site.  Lacking the
ability to use MicroFocus tools, I wrote a simple COBOL program to read
the indexed file sequentially and write the records to a flat file.
However, the open statement in my program is unsuccessful; I am
receiving a file status of 39.  Does anyone know what this return code
indicates?

If anyone can tell me what I need to do to be able to open the file
successfully in my program, or read the data in the compressed file any
other way, I would be most appreciative.

Thanks in advance for your assistance.

Sincerely yours,

Barbara Weber
baweber@hotmail.com


Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

"You have tried to access a file that is not compatible with the structure
of files under the current release of your software. This could be because
the file was created either under a different operating system or under a
previous version of your current system.

Resolution:

You should create a new copy of the file which has the correct structure."

- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00

- **References:** `<8nroua$rl2$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <8ns2t5$87k$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <8ns4st$1pv$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Wow. That resolution sure was helpful.

Kind of like the old MVS manuals. Thousands of different system codes, each
with a resolution of "Correct problem and re-run".

The best thing I ever read in a manual was in an IBM programmer's guide for
FORTRAN explaining the use of the DATA statement:
"The primary purpose of the DATA statement is to give names to constants;
instead of referring to pi as 3.141592653589793 at every appearance, the
variable pi can be given that value with a DATA statement and used instead
of the longer form of the constant. This also simplifies modifying the
program, should the value of pi change."

IBM's FORTRAN people didn't foresee any Year 2000 issues but at least they
were ready for a change in pi.

#### ↳ Re: meaning of file status 39

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nsejv$5mo$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<8nroua$rl2$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
I think that you have one of the easiest FS=39 cases to solve.  From the
information that you gave us, I think you PROBABLY have a Select/Assign
clause with something like:

 Select abc Assign XYZ
    Organization Sequential
     File status WS-field.

Change this to:

 Select abc Assign XYZ
   Organization Indexed
    Access Sequential
   Record Key FD-Key
    File Status WS-field

You can then process this indexed file sequentially - without lying to it as
to what its organization is.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: meaning of file status 39

- **From:** baweber313@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nu8i0$nhu$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<8nroua$rl2$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <8nsejv$5mo$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Hello All,

almost exactly as you suggested.  The difference is an extra
statement: "lock mode manual".  Ken, I created the input file myself by
running the program that creates it in production, where the file that
is created is used successfully by other programs.  Icccpiasabird, the
access mode is different in that all other programs accessing the file
use dynamic, whereas I am using sequential.

So, I have all this confidence, and I appear to be doing what I should,
yet the open error continues to be a problem.

I'll let you know what works.

Thanks for your time!

Barbara

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: meaning of file status 39

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nuh7u$7tb$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<8nroua$rl2$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <8nsejv$5mo$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net> <8nu8i0$nhu$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
The two remaining "most logical" causes for FS=39  are:

1) The record layout under your FD does NOT really match the record layout
(as far as length, fixed/variable, key location, etc) from the way the file
was originally created.

2) You are pointing to a "part" of the file and not the full file - however,
you relate your external file-name (in the "ASSIGN" clause) to the physical
file on disk.

  ***

Final thought.  If all you really want is to convert an indexed file to a
(record or line) sequential file, you should look in the Micro Focus product
that you have for a utility called "WFL".  This will do it for you.
```

It is very hard, if not impossible, to learn a language without actually
using it.  And with the amount of time you would spend, it is worth
while spending some money to buy a good teaching text.  Thane Hubbel is
a frequent contributor to this forum and his book includes a Cobol
compiler.  The title is something like "Learn Cobol in 24 hours", check
the FAQ.

## Thread: XML and COBOL — 10 this month / 11 total

_Topics: [`Web, XML, modern integration`](topics.md#web)_

### XML and COBOL

- **From:** bdoyle_deja@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8nui5n$416$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
I am using Merant/MicroFocus Net Express 3.1 and
want to be able to read/write in XML

I have done the MSXML thing, invoking methods and
accessing my data through COM, but I figure there
may be a better way.

Has anybody used the XML ODBC method from Merant
yet? Are there examples of using it anywhere?

Has anybody tried any other products, such as XML
Junction? What I am looking for is something that
will easily move data between the XML and COBOL
data structures, as well as something that will
help to define those relationships if possible,
and XML Junction looks like an option.

I am also considering putting more time into
further developing the MSXML approach, so I am in
the "weigh the alternatives" stage.

Thanks,

Barry





Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

<a id="t-91dc8a3fc2"></a>
## Thread: Merant/Micro Focus boneheaded 'support?' — 32 this month / 83 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

_Thread started [2000-07](2000-07.md#t-91dc8a3fc2)._

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Merant/Micro Focus boneheaded 'support?'

- **From:** "Timothy O. Nicholson" <kf4rtx@nospam.nc.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T02:41:48+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MLlo5.55990$rd1.10348429@typhoon-news1.southeast.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<K11b5.1455$h67.547060@news2.mia> <396D4368.399A414@home.com> <8kk4up$m7p$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk>`

```
I would like to throw in another coal on this fire.  The company for which I
work develops software for Wintel systems (mainly 95 & 98) using the
MicroFocus line of COBOL.  We have been using the 16-bit 3.2 for quite some
time, only because we had a bit of bad luck with the bugs found in Net
Express 2.0.  Finally we were convinced that NE 3.0 was as stable as the
16-bit 3.2 platform, so we switched.  Now we find that the runtime licensing
fees make it such that we could not sell our product to our customers
without jacking the prices through the roof, which would put us out of
business!  You say Merant isn't going out of business, Paddy, but let me
tell you this.  Merant is obviously focusing their marketing on the large
companies and thumbing their noses at the small guys.  Your company's
runtime fees are going to cause small- and middle-sized software developers
to go to Java, VB, VC, Fujitsu COBOL or some other language where runtime
fees do not exist.  Fortunately we worked out a deal where we would not be
charged runtime fees as long as we stay with 3.0.  But eventually, we'll be
in the same boat as we were with the 3.2 16-bit version - outdated and
behind technologically.  Please tell your management to stop charging such
outlandish runtime fees!

Timothy Nicholson
Project Manager
Atlatl, Inc.
http://www.accu-rater.com


"Paddy Coleman" <paddy.coleman@merant.com> wrote in message
news:8kk4up$m7p$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk...
> James,
>
> I am afraid software protection is outside my control <g>.  I have
forwarded
> your comments to our Product Marketing people though.
>
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

I found the "full" MERANT stuff from today.  It is at:

 http://www.merant.com/resource_center/investors/press2.asp

Of particular interest to this group (I suspect) is the following,

"Cobol revenues decreased at a faster than expected rate, and this decline
could continue. This phenomenon was experienced across the industry, as other
enterprise software companies have recently reported shortfalls associated
with mainframe revenues. We will be reducing our cost base in Cobol to ensure
future profitability for this revenue stream."

PERSONALLY, I think that last sentence says a LOT about what you can expect
in the future for both NetExpress and MainframeExpress.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: MERANT licence fees

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<K11b5.1455$h67.547060@news2.mia> <396D4368.399A414@home.com> <8kk4up$m7p$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk> <MLlo5.55990$rd1.10348429@typhoon-news1.southeast.rr.com> <8ntgoq$4g3$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk>`

```
From a MERANT press release published today,

"August 22, 2000 04:12 AM

LONDON, Aug 22 (Reuters) - British software tools supplier Merant Plc MRNT
said on Tuesday it would begin a share buy-back programme and consider a
number of strategic alternatives after reporting a first quarter loss.
 ...
 "The company is considering a number of strategic alternatives to maximise
shareholder value," Merant said in a statement.
 ...
Merant is shifting its focus from traditional Cobol (Common Business Oriented
Language) software products to its new Egility e-business software -- which
allows companies to mix computer applications programmed in different
languages.

But it has been unable to replace services as fast as expected, with demand
for its traditional products vanishing at a rapid pace.

Merant's total first quarter Cobol revenue fell 24 percent, and Cobol licence
fees dropped 43 percent. But its Egility business grew seven percent and now
made up nearly two-thirds of its business, Merant said. "


  ***
I found the full press release at:

 http://moneycentral.msn.com/scripts/webquote.dll?iPage=news&Symbol=MRNT

but that page MAY require a "sign-on"
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: MERANT licence fees

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<KIPo5.5198$OE4.34691@news1.atl>`
- **References:** `<K11b5.1455$h67.547060@news2.mia> <396D4368.399A414@home.com> <8kk4up$m7p$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk> <MLlo5.55990$rd1.10348429@typhoon-news1.southeast.rr.com> <8ntgoq$4g3$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk> <8nuifj$dsn$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I don't know the demographics of Merant's COBOL customer base, but if
any sizable proportion of it is independent developers, I can surely
understand why "demand for its traditional products [is] vanishing at
a rapid pace."  Borland made a *killing* selling cheap Pascal compilers.
I don't know about everybody else, but paying several thousand dollars
for a compiler, when most every other compiler can be bought for several
hundred dollars, is a pretty tough pill to swallow.  And you can upgrade
Microsoft's whole Visual Studio (every two or three years) for less than
the yearly maintenance cost of Net Express.

Don't get me wrong, I like Net Express, and I would like to see Merant
make big bucks with it and keep it current.  But when their customers
are constantly complaining about how expensive Net Express is, that
is Not Good.  Personally, I have had many more bugs with Microsoft's
Visual Studio as Merant Micro Focus products.

I own Fujitsu COBOL 4.x and 5.0, but the documentation and operation
seem very non intuitive to me, so I haven't used it much.  And Mickey
Mouse things like having to uninstall the previous version before
installing an upgrade are pretty lame.  But I love not having to deal
with those #@#$%%$@#*@ aggravating response keys, and it's much less
expensive than NE.  I tried the 4.2 conversion MF to Fujitsu converter,
and was hugely unimpressed.  The one with 5.0 may be better, but I have
not tried it.  In my opinion, if Fujitsu adapted their compiler to
compile Micro Focus syntax such as 78 level constants, etc., even
without Panels, such that you could compile most programs directly,
they might hurt Net Express sales, if they haven't already.  For me,
conversion is too much trouble at this time.  NE compiles my old MF
programs virtually unchanged, and they work.  The conversion effort
would have cost more than the $2500 upgrade to NE.  But if I didn't
have a large code base, it might be a different story.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: MERANT licence fees

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** "Ken Mullins" <**Ken**Mullins**@**mindspring.com** remove **'s>
- **Date:** 2000-08-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol

I personally think MF/MERANT have made some very bad business decisions...I
am one of those independent developers...No-one from MF/MERANT asked the
developers about bundling in all the different options currently in
NetExpress...I develop code using native apis, so I have no need for Dialog
System for example...It use to be a separate product, but now is included
whether I plan on using it or not...Lots of other add-ons to NE that I do
not use or need...If these products were separated back out from the NE
IDE/Compiler product, then they could lower the price of the compiler and
annual maintenance, putting it more in line with other compilers and
products...

kenmullins

It's not just compilers that "bundle" products like this. Mercator Software
(formerly TSI) used to separately license Mercator (mapper), the Type Tree
Collection, the ODBC Database Adapter and Sytem editors; now it's all one
product. What I need used to license for about $3000; now I'd have to pay
$5500 (and a higher maintenenace) for the package.

Bummer.

MCM

- **References:** `<K11b5.1455$h67.547060@news2.mia> <396D4368.399A414@home.com> <8kk4up$m7p$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk> <MLlo5.55990$rd1.10348429@typhoon-news1.southeast.rr.com> <8ntgoq$4g3$1@hyperion.mfltd.co.uk> <8nuifj$dsn$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net> <KIPo5.5198$OE4.34691@news1.atl> <cuUo5.1244$Hs.7472@newsfeed.slurp.net> <39a44064.76388857@207.126.101.100>`

```
Do you know how compatible the Fujitsu File Handler is with Merant's?
What I mean is that Merant's FH supports every Cobol file function
(OPEN, READ, WRITE, DELETE, etc.).  We currently have a product
developed around this concept that allows you to access MVS datasets /
members / subroutines directly from Merant debugger products - Animator,
NE, MFE, etc. - all with no coding changes to existing source.  It is a
direct competing product to their MFA, which does some of the same
things.  We are looking to support a different Vendor, as Merant is
definitely not what it used to be.

Some more questions for you:

How do you specify a user-written FH in Fujitsu?
Where can I learn more about how it works?
Does it require Source code changes to use?

As you can see, I am VERY interested in this.

Thanks for your help!
```

COBOL and has its very own manual.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: MERANT licence fees

_(thread depth: 9)_

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2000-08-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20000824172458.26431.00000624@nso-mf.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<39a44064.76388857@207.126.101.100>`

```
In article <39a44064.76388857@207.126.101.100>, thaneH@softwaresimple.com
(Thane Hubbell) writes:

>
>The callable external file handler exists.  EBCDIC is supported
>internally, but I have not tried it with a recent version in a file.
>I don't think this exists yet, but I'll check.
>

EBCDIC is supported in one of 3 ways that I no of:
   1)  XCODESET.DLL/LIB - a callable external to convert sections of 
           a record from EBCDIC to ASCII, This works reasonably well.
           I have run into some 'opportunities' when attempting to translate
           a single character.

   2) DATATOOL - has the ability to read an EBCDIC file, matched to 
           a copybook layout, and translate all fields intelligently from
EBCDIC 
          to ASCII.

  3)  DATATOOL - again , related to direct from tape processing,  for an 
           additional licensing fee you can get an add-on the allows DATATOOL
          to read directly from tape/cart media and handle EBCDIC/ASCII 
          translation.

My personal preference would be for the runtime to deal with the translation 
on the fly based on the information provided in the FD and configuration
section
relative to the CODE-SET of the file and the internal COLLATING SEQUENCE.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: MERANT licence fees

_(thread depth: 11)_

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2000-08-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20000825132434.11609.00000652@nso-cc.aol.com>`

```
In article <8o48u7$vo3$1@news.chatlink.com>, "Lucas, Todd"
<Todd.Lucas@C-Cubed.net> writes:

>Are there any plans for Fujitsu to implement a CALLFH like compiler
>directive to allow a user-written external file handler?
>
>

It was my understanding that the CBL_ routines that use byte_stream 
type file access are from the Micro-Focus EXTFH type calls.  I haven't 
any history or experience using these routines , so I cannot speak with
any level of authority on this matter.  As for the ability to access Indexed 
files created by Micro-Focus, I have no clue as to whether the CBL_ routines
have any logic to deal with Indexed file access.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: MERANT licence fees

_(thread depth: 12)_

- **From:** thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2000-08-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39a6bec5.54431572@207.126.101.100>`
- **References:** `<8o48u7$vo3$1@news.chatlink.com> <20000825132434.11609.00000652@nso-cc.aol.com>`

```
I think you misunderstand.  Merant allows you to write your OWN Extfh
- external File handler - that complies with their call standard for
this.  Then at compile time, you can specify which (and I think
multiples are allowed) of the external file handlers to use.  I DO NOT
KNOW if this can be done with regular SELECT/ASSIGN, Open read and
write statements or not, but is suspect that you CAN because of my
experience with the Btrieve file handler.  

Fujitsu also offers an external call interfae to file access routines
for Fujitsu files -- but the won't let you replace the file system
with one of your own, like Merant does.  Todd is asking if in the
future this is going to happen.  I do not know the answer.

- **From:** "Lucas, Todd" <Todd.Lucas@C-Cubed.net>

```
You hit it right on the money Thane.  I want to specify a CALLFH-like
compiler directive and have the run-time system automatically direct ALL
file requests to MY specified external file handler.  I don't want to
make any source code changes to the program to do this - I just want it
to happen automatically.

I'm not saying I want an exact match of the Merant EXTFH processing, I'm
just saying that it would be nice to have the external file handler
functionality in Fujitsu...

Thanks - TL

Todd Lucas

I have been evaluating Fujitsu v5 to replace my old DOS cobol compiler
(MS-Cobol v4.5 - badged MF) and have found the conversion fairly
straightforward (with Flexus sp2 replacing the Screen Section and Flexus
FormPrint replacing the Report Section).

Fujitsu v5 does support the 78 level - my code uses them and
compiles/runs okay. I find the development system easy to use - I am
used to a seperate edit of a source file and then call the compiler
(from a DOS prompt), never did use the MF workbench.

Brian

I agree with what you wrote hereunder.

We own MF Dev. Suite for Unix under SCO and Unixware. The fees are
quite high. We would like to develop some applications for windows but
the fees for upgrading to NE are disuasive.
We also own Fujitsu Cobol 5. But we develop very little with it. We
have to much code for MF and the code is not enough compatible between
Fujitsu and MF. We have too much code developped using the MF tools -
which are very stable - to begin a conversion for Fujitsu and/or
maintain two ranges of applications.

I still wonder why Fujitsu has not implemented two things that would
probably hurt NE sales : a high level of source compatibility for the
MF code AND a file structure compatibility so that Fujitsu could
natively read/write indexed files created with MF generated code.

But I have never heard of plans for that...

Why is comp.lang.cobol preferable over alt.cobol?

<snip>
>   FYI - comp.lang.cobol is "preferable" over alt.cobol (as described in the
> COBOL FAQ).
<snip>

because someone says it.  Perhaps I would not have lived long in
the 3rd Reicht.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: A Listserver for Cobol

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o3nkk$mko$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<sq6c66odt9121@corp.supernews.com> <8nvcqq$82u$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net> <39A5333E.8C17282E@neo.rr.com>`

```
Two reasons:

1) It is read my SIGNIFICANTLY more readers (and is more active).  This is a
"re-enforcing" situation.  You are more likely to find someone posting (or
who has posted) on any specific topic in C.L.C. And because there are more
people posting and responding in C.L.C., it gets even more readers/posters.

2) alt.cobol is *not* "carried" by all ISPs.  This is because you have to go
thru a "voting" process to get a NG with "comp" as the first part.  As part
of that vote (that took place many years ago), there was supposed to be an
end to "alt.cobol".  This has happened with some servers - but not all.

   ***
Certainly ANYONE who wants to keep using alt.cobol (and has an ISP that
supports it) may continue to do so.  However, I think you find a more limited
audience - and a more limited number of threads of potential interest.  If
you use something like www.deja.com and compare the number of posts and
readers in the two groups, I think you will find this to be a true
representation of current usage.
```

<a id="t-de59df468a"></a>
## Thread: Meaning of "Duplicate public name" — 4 msgs

### Meaning of "Duplicate public name"

- **From:** rezeerf@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8ntra0$7aq$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Hi,

hope someone can answer me this:

I'm using NetExpress 3.1 and on doing my release-build i get the
following error messages:

Rebuilding         RELEASE\Rt.exe

ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\RTAUSK99.OBJ(_RTAUSK45)
ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\RTKONTR.OBJ(_RTKONTR)
ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\RTMENVER.OBJ(_RTMENBAU)
ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\rtsegta4.OBJ(_RTSEGTAB)
ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\RTSEGTAB.OBJ(_RTSEGTAB)
ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\RTUNBAR7.OBJ(_RTUNBAR3)
ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\RTUNBAR8.OBJ(_RTUNBAR2)
ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\RTUNBAR9.OBJ(_RTUNBAR2)

All the buildings of the given files in the project are whithout errors,
only during release-build i get the ones above.
I've looke in the online reference at the error-messages and thought i
could be caused by duplicate filenames or write-protection - but,
nothing like that.

Thank you in advance for any suggestions,

alex


Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

#### ↳ Re: Meaning of "Duplicate public name"

- **From:** Danial Clarke <danial.clarke@merant.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39A3A63D.98A736CD@merant.com>`
- **References:** `<8ntra0$7aq$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
The easy one is the last error - you appear to have the entry point
'RTUNBAR2' defined in more than one file.

When you compile a module to as a .OBJ it will have public symbols for all
the entry-points. You'll always get an entry-point with the same name as the
file, plus you'll get one with the name defined in the PROGRAM-ID clause,
and then one for each 'ENTRY "..."' statement. For example if I have a file
called 'FRED.CBL' as follows:

    IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
    PROGRAM-ID. JIM.

    PROCEDURE DIVISION.
    ENTRY "HARRY".
    STOP RUN.

If I compile this to .OBJ the module will have three entry-points; 'FRED',
'JIM' and 'HARRY'. (You can see the public symbols if you specify the -V
option to CBLLINK).

Since your filenames are all different you need to check the PROGRAM-ID
clauses, and any 'ENTRY' statements.

Hope this helps,


Dan.

rezeerf@my-deja.com wrote:

> Hi,
>
…[27 more quoted lines elided]…
> Before you buy.
```

#### ↳ Re: Meaning of "Duplicate public name"

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-23T02:34:31+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39A33801.A800F448@home.com>`
- **References:** `<8ntra0$7aq$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```


rezeerf@my-deja.com wrote:
> 
> Hi,
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> ERROR: (5) Duplicate public name :  RELEASE\RTAUSK99.OBJ(_RTAUSK45)

I can't guarantee that this is the same - but I had a similar problem
back in May when rebuilding a cut-down version of my application to an
executable, (using Version 3.0).

You can delete, (Remove from Project), from the IDE both in the
'application tree' left-hand side, and from the alphabetical list of
source on the right-hand side. Although I deleted under DEBUG mode, when
I did a REBUILD ALL under RELEASE mode, I got a similar problem to
yours. Within RELEASE mode delete those source (objects) 'hanging loose'
in the tree and it worked.

Jimmy
```

<a id="t-6831eda968"></a>
## Thread: COBOL650 source code — 6 msgs

### COBOL650 source code

- **From:** David Essex <dessex@arvotek.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39A280EA.3D940236@arvotek.net>`

```
Hi All,

I am looking for the COBOL650 (COBOL701) compiler source code.

Does anyone have a copy, or know some one who has ?


For those of you who are not familiar with the COBOL650 compiler for
DOS, and the story behind the compiler, I will expand.

The COBOL650 (COBOL701) compiler was written by Robert Eric Noweck, from
Jesup, Georgia, USA. The binary was, and is,  distributed as shareware. 

Robert Eric Noweck died shortly after he completed version COBOL701. 

It was his wish that after is death, the source code would be placed in
public domain. Supposedly the source was to be placed only on the
AV-SYNC BBS in Atlanta, Georgia. 

The AV-SYNC BBS is no more.

So the question is what happened to the source code ?

Since the  BBS no more, I think it would nice to place the source in
public domain on the internet. I think Robert Eric Noweck would have
approved.



David
```

- **From:** SkippyPB <swiegand@neo.rr.com.nospam>

```

enlightened us:

>Hi All,
>
…[27 more quoted lines elided]…
>David

David, there was an ongoing thread in comp.lang.cobol about this.  I
thought I had it, but it turned out I didn't.  I've spent a couple of
days searching the net and cannot find the source code anywhere.  As
you stated, the source was turned over to the BBS you mentioned which
was being run by a guy named Bill Tullis.  I could find no telephone
listing for a Bill Tullis anywhere in Georgia and, as you said, the
BBS is no longer active.

I'm afraid this code may be lost.

Regards,

#### ↳ Re: COBOL650 source code

- **From:** David Essex <dessex@arvotek.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39A33B78.E2D3ECCC@arvotek.net>`
- **References:** `<39A280EA.3D940236@arvotek.net>`

```
Hi All,


Perhaps I should clarify the reason for my posting.

The only place to get the source for the COBOL650 (COBOL701) compiler
would be in either of the following. The AV-SYNC BBS in Atlanta, the
former maintainer of the BBS, Bill Tulllis, or from some one that
actually downloaded the source code from the BBS.

Since the source code is no longer available form the BBS, and it is
unlikely that former maintainer has a copy. Then the only other possible
solution to this quest is, if some one who still has the source code
comes forward.

So assuming that the source was ever on the BBS, there must be some one
on this planet who has a copy and can make it available.


 
David
```

#### ↳ Re: Webmasters == INTELLECTUAL MAGGOTS of computing

- **From:** Craig Bennett <theclyde@mindless.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.www.webmaster,comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol,nyc.food
- **Message-ID:** `<3zakOQpAC4CO78KnlLobhnVrdeko@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<967058665967295@yahoo.com>`

```
>I think that webmasters and web designers are the intellectual maggots
>of computing.  They do the stupidest, most boring types of work -- web
>page/HTML typing is not exactly a rocket science -- while true programmers
>make shitloads of money doing intellectually challenging tasks. We, the 
>object oriented programmers, laugh at you and despise you secretly.

No doubt..  the big difference between technical html knowhow and being an
effective webmaster is the additional of design & layout skills.  Any fool
or their monkey can create a webpage (or newsletter for that matter). It
takes a bit of skill and artistry to create a website that is easy to use
and aesthetically (spelling?) pleasing.  

I know a few hardcore programmers who just flounder when they have to make
a GUI for their application. The programs work, but man - I would not touch
them with a ten foot pole as a user.

Another brick into the fire is that quite often now, the role of webmaster
also encompasses some programming in the form of asp, jscript or cgi (such
as perl).

Craig

touch

> Craig

Please don't feed the trolls.

But you do have a good point; the skills of web designers/masters are often
much much broader than a code monkey. A good web master will have skills in
programming, layout design, GUI design, speed optimisation, search engine
optimisation, image creation/manipulation, editing, information
architecture, information management etc. Having been a coder in the past, I
would never go back. Web design is infinitely more rewarding, stimulating
and enjoyable.

The fact that some programmers look down their noses should just reassure
you that they have very little else in their lives to give them any joy.
```

#### ↳ Re: Webmasters == INTELLECTUAL MAGGOTS of computing

- **From:** jak phost <jackfrost5000@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.www.webmaster,comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol,nyc.food
- **Message-ID:** `<48i8qsg36gf7s1djcaujm88opbdtlmuokb@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<967058665967295@yahoo.com>`

```
I agree with the other people here.  I've programmed, and I've created
web pages and programming by far is the more boring of the two.  Like
someone else here said, creating web pages is more of an art form like
playing guitar, painting, or writing.  Programming has none of
attributes of an artist.  Its hours upon hours of sitting in front of
a computer trying to figure out why the stupid program isn't working
the way you want it to.  And completing a program has little of the
gratification that comes with creating web pages.  So what, you wrote
a program (and like someone else said, the GUI prob sucks unless an
artist was hired) and who is going to see it?  The 200 people that
downloaded in from www.download.com (a web site created by webmasters
to push your crappy software) or if your lucky, you work for a bigger
company that has the privilege of having your hard work pirated by
thousands.  And unlike software, millions of people can enjoy a web
page,  much like a hit song.  Not because it makes their computer run
faster, or because it allows them to easily partition their hard
drives.  Those people will enjoy it for the sheer beauty of it.  But
then again, being enraptured by a piece of art is something YOU
programmers will prob never experience.

Well, I think I said my piece.  I shouldn't have even wasted my time.

-jak
http://www.headzoo.com

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Webmasters&Programmers=bad design

- **From:** Mike <moon12@erols.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T01:13:27-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.www.webmaster,comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol,nyc.food
- **Message-ID:** `<39A8A375.67FBE10@erols.com>`
- **References:** `<967058665967295@yahoo.com> <MPG.1410bc914ff61c04989781@news.virgin.net> <39a84c43.0@speedtrap.i2k.com>`

```

Neither the "oh-so-hip" webmasters, or the 
"geeky-enough-to-be-god" programmers have
any idea of what good design is.

Who cares how fast a page loads, or how mighty
your database is if the final product looks like
something a camel would regurgitate.

No design sense, misappropriated style guides, and
a total lack of aesthetic or typographical knowledge
make the web an truly ugly experience.

Both of you can go get some design training
or go fuck yourselves.
```

all pretty left-brain. On the other hand, there are plenty of good sites out

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Webmasters&Programmers=bad design

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Diana" <dwag@voicenet.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.www.webmaster,comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol,nyc.food
- **Message-ID:** `<Ed8q5.1895$xL6.85526@news3.voicenet.com>`
- **References:** `<967058665967295@yahoo.com> <MPG.1410bc914ff61c04989781@news.virgin.net> <39a84c43.0@speedtrap.i2k.com> <39A8A375.67FBE10@erols.com>`

```
In real life, I'm a writing teacher.  The MOST IMPORTANT thing for people to
learn is audience awareness.  In my region of the world, for instance, the
number of people on-line is quite low and there is not an assumption that
lots of people are power users.  To our advantage, on-line savvy is pretty
low, so we can appeal to new 'net users every single day.

I've taught some locals how to use the Net, and "flash and dash" designs
confuse and befuddle them.  So in our case, I design for the lowest common
denominator.  Programmers want to poo-poo our lack of fanciness, but our
audience is a) pragmatic and b) low-tech.

Everyday I visit web sites that are put together by people whose only goal
seems to be showing off their "skill."  And much of the time, I click out of
that site in less than a minute.  What you say is so much more important
than how much code you say it with (within reason of course).

If designers and programmers would stop playing the "mine is bigger than
yours is" game, than maybe the internet would start serving our *visitors*
purposes a little better.

Diana
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Webmasters&Programmers=bad design

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** Mike <moon12@erols.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T00:00:00

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Webmasters&Programmers=bad design

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Jim_Kearman" <jkearman@NOmindspring.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.www.webmaster,comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8obp6t$ug8$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<967058665967295@yahoo.com> <MPG.1410bc914ff61c04989781@news.virgin.net> <39a84c43.0@speedtrap.i2k.com> <39A8A375.67FBE10@erols.com> <Ed8q5.1895$xL6.85526@news3.voicenet.com>`

```

Diana <dwag@voicenet.com> wrote in message
news:Ed8q5.1895$xL6.85526@news3.voicenet.com...
> In real life, I'm a writing teacher.  The MOST IMPORTANT thing for people
to
> learn is audience awareness.

I used to do that, and I agree.

> If designers and programmers would stop playing the "mine is bigger than
> yours is" game, than maybe the internet would start serving our *visitors*
> purposes a little better.

When you get out of the boondocks and back to the big city, I'm sure you
head for the nearest bookstore, which also sells a variety of periodicals.
You don't buy a copy of every one of them, right? The Web gives anyone with
a computer, modem and telephone line the chance to express him/herself. The
cultural and stylistic variety is amazing. Whatever your tastes and
abilities you can find something to suit you. The stuff that's good will
endure, the rest will be replaced by what's better. That's as it should be.
Power to the people.
```

<basspro9@bellsouth.net> wrote:

> Anyone heard of it? and know where I can get a copy?
>

> 

You perhaps mean Realia (Computer Associates) Cobol?
The compiler executable has been called realcob.exe since
first introduced.  

Computer Associates activly markets it, it is still the

#### ↳ Re: RealCob

- **From:** Support@ScreenIO.com (Kevin J. Hansen)
- **Date:** 2000-08-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39a54114.231518586@news>`

On Wed, 23 Aug 2000 23:38:20 -0500, "Charles B. Summers"
<basspro9@bellsouth.net> wrote:

>Anyone heard of it? and know where I can get a copy?

You can get more information from the CA website at:

http://www.cai.com/products/realiaii_workbench.htm

Although their marketing is directed towards using it for mainframe
development (develop your code on the PC and upload it to the
mainframe), it's an excellent compiler for PC applications.

Kevin

Norcom - COBOL Programming Tools
http://www.screenio.com

I LARTed (Luser Attitude Readjustment Tool) his spam to
Earthlink/Mindspring. Got back a result which said, in
part:

----
Hello,

Thank you for your report.

The account responsible has been located, terminated and billed for
cleanup costs for violations of our Acceptable Use Policy.

EarthLink/MindSpring does not condone and will not permit abusive
behavior
by its users.  Though no summary judgements will be made, all
complaints
we receive will be actively investigated and action will be taken when
and
where it is appropriate.
-----

You notice his original message has been deleted from the newsgroup. I
imagine Earthlink sent kill notices to 15,000 newsgroups and (I hope)
charged him $1.00 each.

## Thread: Migration to LE from COBOL II — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Migration and conversion`](topics.md#migration)_

### Migration to LE from COBOL II

- **From:** "Kevin Wailes" <kevin.wailes@capgemini.co.uk>
- **Date:** 2000-08-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o2v0f$398$1@taliesin2.netcom.net.uk>`

```
Does anyone know of an easily available tool for scanning loadlibs for VS
Cobol compiled modules?

Ta!

Kevin
```

#### ↳ Re: Migration to LE from COBOL II

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o2v9n$ho0$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<8o2v0f$398$1@taliesin2.netcom.net.uk>`

```
Edge Portfolio Analyzer does what you are talking about - and is mentioned in
the IBM Migration Guide.  If you haven't read that book, then that should be
one of your first steps in any migration.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Migration to LE from COBOL II

- **From:** "Kevin Wailes" <kevin.wailes@capgemini.co.uk>
- **Date:** 2000-08-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o2vo3$3ik$1@taliesin2.netcom.net.uk>`
- **References:** `<8o2v0f$398$1@taliesin2.netcom.net.uk> <8o2v9n$ho0$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Bill

would do the job.

Kevin

news:8o2v9n$ho0$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net...

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Migration to LE from COBOL II

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o373p$cp5$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<8o2v0f$398$1@taliesin2.netcom.net.uk> <8o2v9n$ho0$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net> <8o2vo3$3ik$1@taliesin2.netcom.net.uk>`

```
I believe that there is also a "freebie" on the CBT tape - but I don't easily
have the references for that.  If no one else "jumps in" with the correct
reference, I will check further.

(you could check www.deja.com - for either comp.lang.cobol or the IBM-MAIN
list, but I am not certain what to check for.)

I tend to recommend the EPA (Edge Portfolio Analyzer) because it does a
VARIETY of tasks that are important for an LE migration.  However, I do
believe there are other products that will go thru a PDS of load modules and
find OS/VS COBOL programs - if that is all that you need.

P.S.  You are aware that if you have OS/VS COBOL R2.4 compiled programs that
they are and will continue to be supported (in object code) with LE?  If they
were compiled with NORES, then you would need to re-link-edit them with the
LE link-edit library.  However, if they are compiled RES, they are supported
"fully" with LE.

This does NOT mean that I don't recommend "upgrading" such code so that they
can be maintained and re-compiled with a newer and SUPPORTED compiler.
However, there are lots of people who still don't realize that IBM does
support OS/VS COBOL object code running under LE.
```

a utility in SCLM (part of ISPF) that creates a list of modules called
an archdef from load modules.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Compiling a COBOL program using MicroFocus compiler

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Lhup5.8123$eL4.71680@news4.atl>`
- **References:** `<8o3rav$8ub$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <CIep5.5853$Mw.53865@news2.atl> <8o4d5h$t9a$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
You can input 'compiler directives' into Microsoft COBOL 4.5 (a
Microsoft modified and relabeled version of Micro Focus COBOL 2.x)
several ways, but the simplest way to get a listing is through the
compiler command line.

    COBOL [source-file-name[,[obj-file-name][,[source-list-name]
        [,[object-list-name]]] [directives][;]

Assuming your program source name is PROGRAM.COB, and you want to
create object PROGRAM.OBJ and list file PROGRAM.LST, you can use
the syntax:

    COBOL PROGRAM.COB,PROGRAM.OBJ,PROGRAM.LST;

Object-list-name is the name of the object listing file.  If you want
one, then append that too.  Specifying the source listing name will
create a listing file.  But if you have $NOLIST embedded in your
source file, the listing will be turned off at that point.  You can
turn on the listing again by using $LIST.
```

Hi all,

I am an extreme newbie to COBOL programming, and I have been passed a
project to update it.  This project uses a Visual Basic shell which calls
several COBOL programs which create and copy data.

What I would like to know is if I can pass the directory and file name of my
input and output files which is determined by the Visual Basic program to
these COBOL programs, if so how would it be accomplished?

I thank you in advance for your kind and generous responses.

Regards

Paul
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Passing parameters from a Visual Basic program

- **From:** "Paul Stoner" <stpaul@raex.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o699l$9ft$1@plonk.apk.net>`
- **References:** `<8o5q4p$2hn$1@plonk.apk.net> <8o66e4$mvs$1@news.igs.net>`

```
Yes I do basically is goes as follows:

ShellHandle = Shell("RE01.EXE", vbNormalFocus)

Where ShellHandle is dimensioned Public and Long
RE01.EXE is my COBOL program.

A similar call is made for each program used throughout the process.  Also
each program uses a particular text file.  Now, the way this whole VB/COBOL
program is used is the VB exe is placed in the "working directory" each time
it is needed.  All of the files the COBOL programs use are located in this
working directory.

What I want to be able to do with this is to install this VB/COBOL program
once on the C: drive of the computer.  When it is started a VB form will
come up asking the user the location of the working directory.  Once they
enter this path I want to be able to pass that path on to my COBOL programs
so they know where the files needed are located.

I do hope I have made myself clear.

Thanks for the input

Regards,

Paul

information.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Passing parameters from a Visual Basic program

- **From:** "donald tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8ob0sd$a5s$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<8o5q4p$2hn$1@plonk.apk.net> <8o66e4$mvs$1@news.igs.net> <8o699l$9ft$1@plonk.apk.net>`

```
OK.  What is being done is not calling a subroutine. What is being done is
simply executing the Cobol program using the command line interpreter from
inside Basic, but that still allows you to do what you want.  You will have
to modify the Cobol programs to accept the file name from the command line,
and then insert the filename into the literal. For example, the one below
would be modified to 'ShellHandle = Shell("RE01.EXE MYFILE.DATA",
vbNormalFocus', and the Cobol program modified to pick up "MYFILE.DATA" as a
command line argument.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Passing parameters from a Visual Basic program

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Lucas, Todd" <Todd.Lucas@C-Cubed.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<nZzq5.118$l63.699@newsfeed.slurp.net>`
- **References:** `<8o5q4p$2hn$1@plonk.apk.net> <8o66e4$mvs$1@news.igs.net> <8o699l$9ft$1@plonk.apk.net> <8ob0sd$a5s$1@news.igs.net>`

```
If you are using Micro Focus / Merant Cobol, then you can use the
following verbiage to obtain the command-line that is passed to a Cobol
program (from VB or any other).  I also have a complete example of
calling Cobol DLL's from VB if you would like it.  Just let me know...

Accept parameters from command-line:

Working-Storage Section:
       01  ws-command-line    pic x(255).

Procedure Division Code:
           move spaces                 to ws-command-line.
           accept ws-command-line      from command-line.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Passing parameters from a Visual Basic program

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** Stefan Meyer <meyerst@my-deja.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8ogdvg$5t0$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<8o5q4p$2hn$1@plonk.apk.net> <8o66e4$mvs$1@news.igs.net> <8o699l$9ft$1@plonk.apk.net> <8ob0sd$a5s$1@news.igs.net> <nZzq5.118$l63.699@newsfeed.slurp.net>`

```
Or you just use:
      *-----------------------------------------------------------------
       LINKAGE Section.
      *-----------------------------------------------------------------
       01 CallParam.
           03 CallParamLength  pic 9(4) comp-x.
           03 CallParamLine.
               05 CallParamChar pic x occurs 1 to 999
                                      depending on CallParamLength.

      *-----------------------------------------------------------------
       PROCEDURE DIVISION using CallParam.

The params will be stored in the structure above and you can parse them
very easily doing soemthing like:

            Move 1 to naechst-argv
            perform until naechst-argv > CallParamLength
               add 1 to dIdx
               unstring CallParamLine delimited by space
                   into argv
                   count in argv-laenge
                   with pointer naechst-argv
               end-unstring
               if Argv-Laenge > Argv-Max-Laenge
                *> some error processing
               else
                *> process the datas
               end-if
            end-perform

It works with Merant Cobol but I don't know if it works with other
compilers as well.

HTH - Stefan Meyer

#### ↳ Re: Passing parameters from a Visual Basic program

- **From:** slelliott@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o6nbs$jaf$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<8o5q4p$2hn$1@plonk.apk.net>`

```
Paul,

We routinely call Realia Cobol DLL�s and EXE�s.  I�m not sure what
flavor of Cobol you are using, but I wouldn�t think the VB side would
change.  On the Cobol side though, the way you complile and link may be
different.  I�ll just address the VB side (version 6).

You can Shell to the Cobol program just like you do with any .exe.  VB
has a built-in Shell function, I didn�t find it rich enough to handling
the passing of parameters so you need to turn to the windows API.  I�ve
included the Declare statement below.  As with all API declares, make
sure it�s exact.

The constant are possible return codes from the shell call.

Here�s a brief explanation on the following code.

modcmdCallWithParms_Click() is the public entry point in the program
and simply sets the parameters to be passed, makes the call, and then
evaluates the return code.  Within this sub, strDocName contains the
exe�s path and file name, strParameters contains the parameters you are
passing to the Cobol program (I believe you�re wanting to pass the path
selected from a common dialog control), and then the execution path for
the Cobol exe is in strDefaultDir.

StartDoc is simply the wrapper for the API call.

The only �got ya� you really have to watch for is the way windows looks
for the exe�s when you�re designing vs. the way it does when you make
your VB source into an EXE.  If the DLL�s or EXE�s you are calling are
not located in your app.path, then make sure your �Start In� location
associated with your desktop shortcut to VB is the same as the path to
those DLL�s or EXE�s.  I spent serval hours chasing that gremlin.

I hope this works for you.

Steve Elliott
CGW, Inc.

Option Explicit

Private Declare Function ShellExecute Lib "shell32.dll" Alias _
"ShellExecuteA" (ByVal hwnd As Long, ByVal lpszOp As _
String, ByVal lpszFile As String, ByVal lpszParams As String, _
ByVal lpszDir As String, ByVal FsShowCmd As Long) As Long

Private Declare Function GetDesktopWindow Lib "user32" () As Long

Const SW_SHOWNORMAL = 1

Const SE_ERR_FNF As String = 2
Const SE_ERR_PNF As String = 3
Const SE_ERR_ACCESSDENIED As String = 5
Const SE_ERR_OOM As String = 8
Const SE_ERR_DLLNOTFOUND As String = 32
Const SE_ERR_SHARE As String = 26
Const SE_ERR_ASSOCINCOMPLETE As String = 27
Const SE_ERR_DDETIMEOUT As String = 28
Const SE_ERR_DDEFAIL As String = 29
Const SE_ERR_DDEBUSY As String = 30
Const SE_ERR_NOASSOC As String = 31
Const ERROR_BAD_FORMAT As String = 11

Function StartDoc(DocName As String, Parameters As String, DefaultDir
As String) As Long
    Dim Scr_hDC As Long
    Scr_hDC = GetDesktopWindow()
    StartDoc = ShellExecute(Scr_hDC, "Open", DocName, _
    Parameters, DefaultDir, SW_SHOWNORMAL)
End Function

Public Sub modcmdCallWithParms_Click()
    Dim r As Long, msg As String
    Dim strDocName As String
    Dim strParameters As String
    Dim strDefaultDir As String
    strDocName = "D:\eone4win\vers41\programs\ezlmo.exe"
    strParameters = "         B:Y C:Y D:Y"
    strDefaultDir = "d:\eone4win\vers41\programs"

    r = StartDoc(strDocName, strParameters, strDefaultDir)
    If r <= 32 Then
        'There was an error
        Select Case r
            Case SE_ERR_FNF
                msg = "File not found."
            Case SE_ERR_PNF
                msg = "Path not found."
            Case SE_ERR_ACCESSDENIED
                msg = "Access denied."
            Case SE_ERR_OOM
                msg = "Out of memory."
            Case SE_ERR_DLLNOTFOUND
                msg = "DLL not found."
            Case SE_ERR_SHARE
                msg = "A sharing violation occurred."
            Case SE_ERR_ASSOCINCOMPLETE
                msg = "Incomplete or invalid file association."
            Case SE_ERR_DDETIMEOUT
                msg = "DDE Time out."
            Case SE_ERR_DDEFAIL
                msg = "DDE transaction failed."
            Case SE_ERR_DDEBUSY
                msg = "DDE busy."
            Case SE_ERR_NOASSOC
                msg = "No association for file extension."
            Case ERROR_BAD_FORMAT
                msg = "Invalid EXE file or error in EXE image."
            Case Else
                msg = "Unknown error."
        End Select
        MsgBox msg & "  Contact CGW.", vbExclamation, "Error"
    End If
End Sub

## Thread: OT: VSAM DASD Space Allocation — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files) · [`Off-topic and spam`](topics.md#spam)_

### OT: VSAM DASD Space Allocation

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<FUup5.1048$OE.54914@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`

```
8/25/00

[NOTE: A form of this message appears in the VSAM forum at the MVS help web
site; apologies if you've had to endure it twice].

About eight months ago, I asked at www.mvshelp.com if anyone could suggest a
'VSAM for Dummies'- type tool to enable a calculation of the space
allocations which should be used during an IDCAMS DEFINE of a VSAM dataset.
Several people got me started in the right direction.

I have now developed a piece of software to do this; my intent is to give
this away, hoping the advertising and self-promotional value is something
greater than zero.

But...

I would really appreciate the chance to get two groups of "real users" to
take a look at this before I make it broadly available.

First, I'd like two or three "VSAM naifs" who would be real users: people
who really do not have any good idea of how to at least do a preliminary
sizing of VSAM files. From these people I want "real end-user comments" -
what is not clear, what I should add, subtract, etc.  COBOL programmers new
to the mainframe environment - where you need "pre-create" all  ACCESS IS
RANDOM/DYNAMIC (indexed or relative) files - are actually my primary target.

Second, I would like some true "VSAM experts" to review the documentation
and suggest changes, improvements or corrections. I *know* I have made some
assumptions on the math which I am sure can be improved upon.

The software is a single 53K Windows 32-bit (NT/9x/2K) executable. It does
not require any support DLLs, does not read or write to the system registry
or do any other annoying stuff. To run it, you click on the EXE file twice
in Windows Explorer. To un-install, you delete the file.

When you click the "Doc" pushbutton, it extracts the doc file to a text file
in the same directory where the executable is and launches NOTEPAD to view
it. (All the math details are in the documentation).

What's in it for those who help? Only this: I will put your name in the
documentation as part of the "Thanks To" section.

If you'd like to help, just send an e-mail request to
michael.mattias@gte.net with a return e-mail address. I can attach the file
to the email as an EXE or I can ZIP it if you need to get it past a firewall
or just feel better about it that way.

Thanks,
Michael Mattias
Racine WI
michael.mattias@gte.net

PS: Please let me know which group you are in: 'tyro' or 'wizard;' and if
you want EXE or ZIP delivery.
```
