## Thread: Compiling using Fukitsu COBOL v3.0 — 3 msgs

### Compiling using Fukitsu COBOL v3.0

- **From:** "Paul Stoner" <stpaul@raex.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o6g03$cvc$1@plonk.apk.net>`

```
Hi all

I am trying to compile a COBOL source file using Fujitsu COBOL I believe it
is version 3.0 (COBOL85 ? if that helps?).

How do I create an EXE? HELP!? Please?

Thanks a lot

Regards

Paul
```

#### ↳ Re: Compiling using Fukitsu COBOL v3.0

- **From:** "mike sheehan" <sheehan_mj@iolfree.ie>
- **Date:** 2000-08-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<_Hzp5.882$_3.3366@news.iol.ie>`
- **References:** `<8o6g03$cvc$1@plonk.apk.net>`

```
This works for Fujitsu COBOL 4.0, but, it should work the same...

You can select compile in the editor...

To create an exe:
1. Launch the COBOL Project Manager
2. Create a new folder for the project and source
3. Open a new project by giving it a name and selecting the folder
4. In Project Manager select/highlight the project
5. Select menu Edit... new file
6. An empty executable "New File..EXE" is created - you can change the name
7. Select it and select menu Edit... new folder... COBOL source File
8. Select the new Cobol folder - Select menu Edit... New File
9. You can rename the source file
10. Double click the COB file to edit it: type in your program
11. Once a program is typed in - Select tools ... compile in the editors
menu
12. When you are happy with the program - select the project from the
project menu
13. Select project menu ... build. The application should build ok
14. To run the executable ... double click on the executable file

note: an alternative to going to the menu is once you have highlighted a
file - you can right click and select options available to you......

Hope this is of help.
Mike

## Thread: Compiling in MicroSOFT COBOL 4.5 — 6 msgs

### Compiling in MicroSOFT COBOL 4.5

- **From:** thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2000-08-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39a7c957.122682459@207.126.101.100>`

```
Query - I'm trying to help someone compile.

I want to compile then link with MicroSoft 4.5 - and create a 16 Bit
Windows application, including calls to an external C program in a LIB
file.  

Does anyone have the command lines for compile and link?

Does MicroSoft COBOL 4.5 even create Windows 3.1 executables?
---
Try a better search engine:  http://www.google.com
My personal web site: http://www.geocities.com/Eureka/2006/
```

#### ↳ Re: Compiling in MicroSOFT COBOL 4.5

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@aiservices.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8o8nhh$1802$1@news.hitter.net>`
- **References:** `<39a7c957.122682459@207.126.101.100>`

```
Microsoft COBOL 4.5 is equivalent to Micro Focus COBOL 2.5.

The Micro Focus 2.5 reference manuals do not mention Microsoft
Windows, at all. (OS/2 and Presentation Manager are mentioned.)

I think it unlikely that MS COBOL 4.5 could be used to create
Windows executable, directly. IIRC, MS COBOL 5.0, equivalent to
MF COBOL 3.0, was the first to have the ability to create Windows
executables, directly. (Actually, IIRC, it was called Microsoft COBOL
for Windows, 5.0.)
------------------
Rick Smith

>

```

[posted and mailed]

I located an MSDN article that addresses your request. A copy
of the article is attached to the e-mail only!

The article is titled "How to Pass Parameters Between COBOL
and C". Among the sample code given is a COBOL 4.5 Quickwin
program calling a MS C 6.0 DLL for Windows 3.0. Other samples
include calling C and linking COBOL and C. Code and command
lines for compiling and linking are also provided. (15 pages ).
------------------
Rick Smith

#### ↳ Re: Compiling in MicroSOFT COBOL 4.5

- **From:** jay_moseley@attglobal.net (Jay Moseley, CCP)
- **Date:** 2000-08-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39a7f3f8.6703429@news1.attglobal.net>`

```
On Sat, 26 Aug 2000 13:44:22 GMT, thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane
Hubbell) wrote:

>Query - I'm trying to help someone compile.
>
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>Does MicroSoft COBOL 4.5 even create Windows 3.1 executables?

I have MicroSoft COBOL 5.0, so I don't know how much changed between
the two versions, but here is the blurb for compiling Windows programs
from the included samples.txt file:

======================================================================
WINDOWS API SAMPLES
See Programming for Windows

\cobol\samples\windows\compress.cbl
\cobol\samples\windows\sysmets.cbl
\cobol\samples\windows\wincalc.cbl
\cobol\samples\windows\winhello.cbl

Compile:
  cobol foo target(286) deffile deffiletype"windows" nodll;

Link:
 Static-linked run-time system Windows API:
   link foo+cblwina,,,lcobolw+lcobol+libw/nod/noe;

 Shared run-time system Windows API:
   link foo+cblwina,,,coblibw+coblib+libw/nod/noe;

======================================================================
WINDOWS API CALLING WINDOWS DLL SAMPLE
See Programming for Windows, Chapter 2, and windll.bat

\cobol\samples\windows\winmain.cbl
\cobol\samples\windows\cobdll.cbl

Compile:
  cobol winmain target(286) deffile deffiletype"windows" nodll;
  cobol cobdll target(286) deffile deffiletype"windows" dll;

Explicitly Link:
  link winmain+cblwina,winmain.exe,,lcobolw+lcobol+libw,
 winmain.def/nod/noe;
  link cobdll+cblwinl+libinit,cobdll.dll,,lcobolw+lcobol+libw,
 cobdll.def/nod/noe;
======================================================================

If I can look up specific questions in my manuals, let me know.

Regards,

Jay Moseley, CCP

http://jaymoseley.jumpcomputers.com/
```

===================================================
Windows 3.0 Support (.. of MS Cobol 4.5)
CBLSSEG.DLL    Support DLL used by all COBOL Windows programs
CBLWINB.OBJ    Startup module for batch programs running under Windows
CBLWINC.OBJ    Startup module for QuickWin applications
CBLWING.OBJ    Startup module for COBOL GUI applications
CBLWINL.OBJ    Startup module for Dynamic Link Libraries
COBAPIDW.LIB   Link library for QuickWin applications
COBAPIW.LIB    Link library for GUI applications and DLLs
DWSKEL.DEF     .DEF file for QuickWin applications
LIBW.LIB       Link library for Windows applications
LLIBCEW.LIB    Link library for Windows applications
LDLLCEW.LIB    Link library for Windows DLLs
LIBENTRY.OBJ   Needed for Windows DLLs
WINSTUB.EXE    Needed for Windows applications
RC.EXE         Windows Resource Compiler
RCPP.EXE
RCPP.ERR
SDKPAINT.EXE   Windows Icon, Cursor and Bitmap painter
SDKPAINT.DAT

#### ↳ Re: Compiling in MicroSOFT COBOL 4.5

- **From:** B W Spoor <bwspoor@fcs.eu.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39A94412.ECC171E1@fcs.eu.com>`
- **References:** `<39a7c957.122682459@207.126.101.100>`

```
Thane,

MicroSoft COBOL 4.5 allows you to compile and link 16 bit programs for
Windows 3.0 (ISTR facility introduced in this version) as well as for
OS/2.

This version also allows mixed language programming with MS-C v6 modules
- you need MS-C0BOL v5 to work with MS-C v7 (the reason that I was
looking for a copy of the v5 compiler to test some mixed COBOL/C code).

I still use this compiler for maintaining DOS systems, but have never
tried to create windows programs (well maybe the examples when I first
got it, but ISTR that I never got them to work) and still have the full
manual set etc.

You need to look at Chapter 3 of the "Advanced TRopics and Utilities
Reference". I have emailed some further detailed information direct to
you, so as not to clog the newsgroup with it.

Brian

Any Help is appreciated.

Stephen
procedure TMainForm.Button1Click(Sender: TObject);
var
  pc: pcT;
  cu:cuT;
  sDate:string ;
  aDate : array[0..7] of char;
begin

pc.MibsFlg[0] := 'Y';        // initialize whether mibs pressent
 pc.err:= '000';// ex error code;
 // get common directory for all winMibs applications
 pc.fName := 'e:\Demo\';//'e:\demo\'  ;

 PIBSinit(pc); // initialize mibs     <<== Get error here.
//EAccessViolation with access violation at address 6014FD in module
cblrtss.dll
read Address ...

sDate:= '20000607';
 sDate:= assignFixedString(adate,sDate) ;
 PC.date:= '20000606';
 pc.chgDate:= '20000606' ;//pc.date ;
 pc.pmtDate:= '20000606';//pc.date;
 pc.err:= '000';
CUSTIO(pc,cu);
pc.err:= '000';
end;



unit EDRIOInt;
{ Interface routine for EDRIO.DLL }

interface
 uses

type


  pPCT = ^pcT;
  pcT = Packed Record//PIBS Comm
    Ver: Array[0..5] of char;//string[6];
    verDate:Array[0..7]of char;//sstring[8];
    Prog:Array[0..31]of char;//sstring[32];
    err:Array[0..2]of char;//sstring[3];
    first:Array[0..0] of Char;// first Call
    mibsFlg:Array[0..0] of Char;// Y mibs is present
    initFlg:Array[0..0] of Char;// y means Initialize new files
    rand:  integer; //random number
    filST:Array[0..4]of char;//sstring[5];
    f1:Array[0..0] of Char;
    date:Array[0..7]of char;//sstring[8];
    Time:Array[0..3]of char;//sstring[4];
    fCmd:Array[0..1]of char;//sstring[2];  //File Command
    fName:Array[0..23]of char;//sstring[24]; //File Name
    nAcct:Array[0..9]of char;//sstring[10];  // next accout Number
    nPat:Array[0..9]of char;//sstring[10];  //Next Pat Record
    debug:Array[0..1]of char;//sstring[2];  //debug flag
    lCls :Array[0..7]of char;//sstring[8];   //last closed date
    qName:Array[0..23]of char;//sstring[24]; //Query Comarea Name
    qAcct:Array[0..11]of char;//sstring[12];  //AccountNumber(num & Type)
    qPat:Array[0..9]of char;//sstring[10];   //Pat number
    qClm:Array[0..3]of char;//sstring[4];  //Claim number
    qCTyp:Array[0..0] of Char;   //Current account type index
    qCmd:Array[0..0] of Char;    //Command
    aCmdS:Array[0..0] of Char;    //sub command
    qFunc:Array[0..0] of Char;    //Function
    qMod:Array[0..0] of Char;    //Mode
    qFuncS:Array[0..0] of Char;  //Sub Function
    f2:Array[0..7]of char;//sstring[8];       //Flags to be later enumerated
    pmtDate:Array[0..7]of char;//sstring[8];  //payment date
    chgDate:Array[0..7]of char;//sstring[8];  //charge date
    msg:Array[0..53]of char;//sstring[54];      //msg line
    f3:Array[0..59]of char;//sstring[60];       //Function Key definitions
    paths:Array[0..959]of char;//sstring[960];    //file paths
    open:Array[0..79]of char;//sstring[80];     //file open Flag
    f4:Array[0..71]of char;//sstring[72];       //filler
    apptlogN:Array[0..23]of char;//sstring[24];  // appt log file name
    f5 :Array[0..33]of char;//sstring[34];        //filler name
    end;
   PnrT= ^nrT ;
   nrT= Packed Record // Name Record;
    Name:Array[0..23]of char;//sstring[24];  //last,First,m,Title
    acct:Array[0..19]of char;//sstring[20];
    pat:Array[0..19]of char;//sstring[20];
    bir:Array[0..7]of char;//sstring[8];  //birth ccyymmdd
    reg:Array[0..5]of char;//sstring[6]; ://registration date
    sex:string[1];
    st:string[1]; //status
    loc:Array[0..7]of char;//sstring[8] ;//location
    end;
  PcuT= ^cuT;
  cuT= Packed Record  //customer record
    Name:Array[0..23]of char;//sstring[24];
    cuStr:Array[0..1095]of char;//sstring[255];//[1096];
    end;


{  PCoinsRec = ^TCoinsRec;
  TCoinsRec = record
    Quarters,
    Dimes,
    Nickels,
    Pennies: word;
  end;
 }
{$IFNDEF EDRIOLIB}
{ Declare function with export keyword }
Procedure PIBSINIT(var pc:pcT); stdCall;
Procedure PIBSEXIT(var pc:pcT);  stdCall;
Procedure NAMEIO(var pc:pcT;var  nr:nrT); stdCall;
Procedure CUSTio(var pc:pcT;var cu:cuT);Stdcall;
{$ENDIF}

implementation

{$IFNDEF EDRIO}
{ Define the imported function }
Procedure PIBSINIT; external 'edrio.dll' name 'PIBSINIT';
Procedure PIBSexit; external 'edrio.dll' name 'PIBSexit';
Procedure NAMEio;   external 'edrio.dll' name 'NAMEio';
Procedure CUSTio;   external 'edrio.dll' name 'CUSTio';
{$ENDIF}

end.
```

<a id="t-a781a7f1f0"></a>
## Thread: Dynamic memory allocation... — 3 msgs

### Dynamic memory allocation...

- **From:** "Ingeborg Coorde" <Ingeborg.Coorde@yucom.be>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<hwC9zMDEAHA.297@btbntsys4>`

```
Hi,

I have a question regarding dynamic memory allocation in COBOL.

I know you can use the LE CEE??? modules in COBOL for mailframe
to dynamically allocate memory.

My question : is there an equivalent on PC to do the same ???

Thanx in advance for your response ...

Tom Verbruggen
Belgium
```

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic memory allocation...

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<CPdq5.744$g%3.163735@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`
- **References:** `<hwC9zMDEAHA.297@btbntsys4>`

```
Under Windows, you can use the API calls GlobalAlloc or HeapAlloc.

Under 'pure' MS-DOS, there is an int x'21' function you can use, but if you
already have a DOS program running, unless you have made arrangements in
your
program to return unused memory to the OS, your program already has all the
system memory (except you can't reach it).

Under MS-DOS, what I have seen done is to load a TSR - which does nothing
except grab a bunch of memory - BEFORE starting the using program; then the
TSR can respond to an interrupt with the address of that memory and the
calling program can use it.  OpTech Sort for MS-DOS and BTrieve for MS-DOS
work this way.
```

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic memory allocation...

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8oc1rq$mb4$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<hwC9zMDEAHA.297@btbntsys4>`

```
Which COBOL compiler are you using on the PC?  Some of them provide
"callable" memory management routines.  I know that Micro Focus (pre-MERANT)
provided *some* CEE-emulation routines, so you ;may be able to port existing
IBM mainframe code directly to the PC.  If you are a Mainframe Express (or
even still a Workbench) customer, check your documentation for which
CEE-routines are supported.

P.S.  NetExpress *also* provides its own memory management routines.  If you
happen to be using IBM VisualAge COBOL, then I think you could use EXEC CICS
GETMAIN/FREEMAIN to do this even in "otherwise non-CICS" applications.
However, I won't swear to that.
```

Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month
Last-Modified: 2000/07/02
Version: 3.01
URL: http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Sunday, July 2, 2000

The COBOL FAQ has moved.  To view the most recent version, please check out the
web version at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send private
email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

The following is Appendix B.5 - Changes to create Version 3.01

 o Recorded new location of the FAQ
 o Updated some old (bad) web links
 o Added some new COBOL books (particularly those written or contributed to - by
frequent comp.lang.cobol submitters).
 o Added information about LegacyJ (PERCobol) and changed the company name from
"Synkronix" to "LegacyJ".
 o Updated information for changes in product and company names and addresses
where available
 o Updated the URLs for downloading the draft of the next COBOL Standard and
CobCy
 o Started the Samples/Example Section
 o Tried to get rid of most specific references to Windows/NT, Windows/95,
Windows/98, and Windows/2000.
 o Added links to Xinotech
 o Added information on Norcom and GUI ScreenIO
 o Added (back) information on Wang
 o Added (back) information on the Tiny-COBOL ('74 Standard) project
```

## Thread: Tiny Cobol 0.1.5.5 released — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Open-source COBOL (GnuCOBOL, OpenCOBOL, TinyCOBOL)`](topics.md#open-source)_

### Tiny Cobol 0.1.5.5 released

- **From:** Rildo Pragana <rpragana@acm.org>
- **Date:** 2000-08-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8ogb4o$2d5$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
It is available the new release 0.1.5.5 of the Tiny Cool compiler.

This minor release contains some bugs fixes, some code enhancements
including updates to the PERFORM statements. With this release, all
PERFORM statements are functional and conform to the COBOL 85 standard.

Tiny Cobol is a free cobol compiler under GPL license. It can be found
at http://tiny-cobol.sourceforge.net
Mirror inquiries are welcome.
```

Hi everybody, in january I will start my first job as COBOL-programmer on an
IBM-Machine (AS400).
For now I am a programmer with 0.5 year experience in COBOL-programming and
that only on a Bull DPS-9000.

Is there anybody who can tell me something about the special things of COBOL
on AS400 and DB2? Are there any good courses in Germany? Are there any books
that could help me?
```

- **From:** praul@cegi.no-spam.net

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<8om277$37k$13$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
Programming in COBOL/400
James Cooper, Nancy Stern, Robert A. Stern
ISBN: 0-471-12722-1
$66.75

Great book on AS/400 COBOL. 

There is also one in the RANDE series from IBM, but you might want to  use
that after you have some experience in the AS/400 Environment. 

Also, John Ross runs a timeshared AS/400 system with COBOL on it. I think
the cost is something on the order of $15/month, and it is a  very well
run little system. 

www.Netshare400.com  I think...

-Paul

#### ↳ Re: AS400

- **From:** thomaskine@my-deja.com
- **Date:** 2000-08-31T18:14:13+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8om78v$2fi$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<8om277$37k$13$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
Probably the biggest thing to look at on an AS/400 are externally-
described files.  On the 400, everything, including both data and
screens are objects.  Files are described with a language called Data
Description Specifications (DDS) and compiled.  Metadata from the
compiled object can then be copied into all programs using the object.

You will see statements like this in the file section of AS/400 COBOL.

01 REPORTING-PERIOD-REC.
   COPY DDR-ALL-FORMATS OF RPPF.

This copies the metadata for a file named RPPF into your program
replacing all underscores in field names "_" with hyphens "-".

There are a variety of formats to the COPY DDx statements, which you
will find documented in the AS/400 Cobol manuals.

You will also find some differences in how AS/400 handles screens,
because they are objects described by metadata.  Additionally, AS/400
screens can contain "subfiles", which are constructs used to describe
repeating groups of data -- very similar to grids in visual programming
languages.  It will likely take you some time to learn how to use these.

The IBM AS/400 Cobol manuals do a good job of discussing file and
screen handling and give good examples to work from.

Good luck.



Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

---

Sorry but I don't understand the question. Do you want to know if COBOL can
allocate memory or whether it can exchange addressable memory with other
routines. The answer to both is yes. The details are documented in the
"Object COBOL Programmer's Guide to Writing Programs - Chapter 10:
Interfacing and Mixed-language Programming"

> compatible with the version of microfocus 4.1 has the feature of the
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…

- **References:** `<8olsii$ki3$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <zUur5.145680$c5.3912692@news2.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
Thanks Oscar! But I want more details. I mean the dynamic allocation of
memory during calls of the routine C++, not addressable memory
exchange. I learned that the early versions of the standards  before
ANSI X3.23b-1993 don't support the feature of the dynamic memory
allocation (see
http://directory.google.com/Top/Computers/Prgramming/Languages/Cobol/Sta
ndards/). So I want to know whether our current version of the
Microfocus Cobol uses the early ANSI standard of Year1993 or later.

Currently, our cobol programe calls a routine C++ to process XML
documents and return the results back. The problem is that the system
is often deadly locked due to lack of the memory when several big XML
documents are processed. We doubt it is caused by our cobol environment
can not allocate new memory to the routines written in other languages
like C++ to process big document. Is it right?

By the way, where can I find the document "Object Cobol Programmer's
Guide to Writing Programs"? I only find "Object Cobol Programmer's
Guide" in the MicroFocus Cobol document folder.

Waiting your answer. Thanks!

COBOL can
> allocate memory or whether it can exchange addressable memory with
other
> routines. The answer to both is yes. The details are documented in the
> "Object COBOL Programmer's Guide to Writing Programs - Chapter 10:
> Interfacing and Mixed-language Programming"
>
> <quazhi@my-deja.com> wrote in message news:8olsii$ki3
$1@nnrp1.deja.com...
> > Does anyone know whether the HP microfocus cobol version B.13.35
> > compatible with the version of microfocus 4.1 has the feature of the
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>


Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/
Before you buy.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Dynamic memory allocation in Microfocus cobol

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-31T13:18:51-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8om7h2$li8$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<8olsii$ki3$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <zUur5.145680$c5.3912692@news2.rdc1.on.home.com> <8om5ou$mb$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
There is something VERY wrong in this post.  Dynamic Memory allocation is
*not* a part of *any* existing COBOL Standard (before or after 1993).  It
*is* a part of the PROPOSED (draft) Standard - but is very definitely NOT a
part of ANSI X3.23b-1993.

MERANT (previously Micro Focus) does provide some callable cbl_ routines for
dynamic memory allocation, if that is what you are asking about. (BUT these
are very definitely "extensions" - not Standard).

Many existing compilers (MERANT among them) also include "USAGE POINTER" data
items - AGAIN as an extension to the current Standard.  These too will be
part of the NEXT Standard (when/if it is approved).

Finally, as I understand your REAL problem, you are finding that your
application "fails" because of memory allocations - and you don't know who
*is* getting this memory.  If that is the real problem, then it is true (and
always has been) that the COBOL run-time system may WELL "acquire" storage to
accomplish some tasks.  Among other things, if your COBOL program calls other
COBOL programs - without canceling them when done, then such storage is
acquired via the first DYNAMIC access to the subprogram and never released.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Dynamic memory allocation in Microfocus cobol

- **From:** "Oscar T. Grouch" <dustbin@home.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-31T18:52:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9Qxr5.146888$c5.3923245@news2.rdc1.on.home.com>`
- **References:** `<8olsii$ki3$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <zUur5.145680$c5.3912692@news2.rdc1.on.home.com> <8om5ou$mb$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
http://support.merant.com/websupport/docs/microfocus/books/oc41books/prpubb.
htm

<quazhi@my-deja.com> wrote in message news:8om5ou$mb$1@nnrp1.deja.com...

> memory during calls of the routine C++, not addressable memory

## Thread: What is Merant's Personel Cobol Window 3.1 ver1.0?? — 2 this month / 6 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### What is Merant's Personel Cobol Window 3.1 ver1.0??

- **From:** michael <ufaccuwa@ms4.hinet.net>
- **Date:** 2000-08-31T23:13:56+08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<39AE7634.F0C95216@ms4.hinet.net>`

```
Hi all,

     The last version of MF Cobol I knew is version 3.2.4. Now I am
completely out of touch. Is Personel Cobol the equivlant to MF Cobol
version 4.0 and what is the latest MF Cobol version anyway? I am totally
confused!

Would anyone be kind enough to point me in the right direction.

Thanks

Michael
```

#### ↳ Re: What is Merant's Personel Cobol Window 3.1 ver1.0??

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-31T11:31:52-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8om18g$1l4$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<39AE7634.F0C95216@ms4.hinet.net>`

```
First check out www.merant.com for complete details on all the MERANT COBOL
(and other) products.

However, "Personal COBOL" is still available, but I believe is
"strategically" replaced by their "Academic" products.  This is a
(relatively) limited product.

The current "strategic" MERANT/Micro Focus COBOL products are (with
APPROXIMATE target audiences):

    Net Express V3.1 (for PC development AND deployment of applications)
    Server Express (primarily run-time support for UNIX-like production
systems)
    Object  COBOL Developer Suite (development environment for UNIX and LINUX
environments)
    Mainframe Express (for PC development of IBM mainframe targeted
applications)

P.S.  FYI - all of this is outlined in a little more detail in the COBOL FAQ
at:
 http://cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
```

<a id="t-952471d616"></a>
## Thread: MERANT responds to AnswerLab and other "support" comments — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### MERANT responds to AnswerLab and other "support" comments

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-31T13:09:04-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8om6ui$4ph$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I received the following from one of my "usually reliable sources" at MERANT
concerning several of the issues that have appeared recently in
comp.lang.cobol.  This information does reply to some "specific" comments in
previous notes that I sent on - and which I am NOT repeating here.  However,
I think this note can be read (and understand) without seeing exactly which
note(s) are being responded to.

Note:  I do *not* "vouch" for any of this - but do consider the source as
someone who would know about this - if anyone does.

"Hi, Bill.

Thanks for passing on the note.  I have asked <other managers> to respond
regarding the Net Express v3.1 amnesty program and the stated availability
notification that was to be made.  I will try to address the issues related
to AnswerLab and other issues related to Support in the note.  Can I ask you
to pass them along to the original forum in answer to the message.

In preface, this year has seen many changes to the SupportNet area of our
web site.  Part of these changes were the rationalization of the different
web services originally provided by Micro Focus and Intersolv as we merged
them together and restructured ourselves into the type of Support operation
we wanted to be to, in our view, provide the best services to our customers.
Another part was so we could offer new and expanded services to make our web
support services more useful to our customers.  In May of this year, we
brought up that new view of those Support web services in the new SupportNet
Online site (www.merant.com/SupportNet).  This new site merged several of
the past functions that we had into fewer and, hopefully, easier to use
features and introduced a number of new services (such as the ability to
report problems to us and to be able to view the records of all of the new
problems that you, or your company, had reported, the new SupportNet
Knowlegebase, etc.).

One of the new services planned was a revamped AnswerLab that would not only
use a later version of the underlying software and serve it from a much more
reliable server, but would also open that service up to users of all of the
MERANT products, not just the Micro Focus branded products.  In revamping
the AnswerLab, we had a problem because of the different login mechanisms
for the (old) AnswerLab and the new SupportNet site, which had it's own
integrated login process and which tied the user login registrations with
our Call Tracking system and, ultimately, with our master customer
information system.  The previous AnswerLab login process used a standalone
database which could not be connected to these other, central, login
services.  So, we had to make a choice as to how to move forward.  We did
what we felt was the obvious solution, which was to shift the AnswerLab
registration to the, now, standard SupportNet Login registration process, so
only a single login would be needed for all SupportNet web services.  This
means that all of the past AnswerLab users would need to re-register for
using the SupportNet Registration process to be able to get to the new
AnswerLab.  Since the old AnswerLab had too much valuable information to
just take it away, we have made it a read-only resource for our customers.
Yes, that does mean that you can't post messages to it any longer (those
need to be posted to the new AnswerLab service), but we have opened up the
access to the old AnswerLab to anyone who has an old AnswerLab login ID, to
anyone accessing it through their SupportNet login ID, or to anyone who uses
an (old AnswerLab) ID and Password of GUEST.

The writer of the <one of the original> messages was right in that he was
given access to the AnswerLab for the past year, even though he let his
product maintenance
lapse.  Until now, we did not have the ability to link the two systems
together.  With the new structure, the registration to access all of the
SupportNet services are tied to the central customer information and
maintenance status is checked automatically.  Yes, this means that should
your maintenance lapse (after a small grace period), your access to the
SupportNet services will also lapse until the maintenance is restored.  When
the writer's original maintenance lapsed, we were transitioning our
maintenance renewal process from one group to another within the company.
For a time, during that transition, some renewal notifications were not sent
out.  However, since the completion of that transition (to a dedicated
maintenance sales and renewal staff), we have been much better at getting
renewal notices out early enough to prevent maintenance lapses.  Any
questions on maintenance prices, renewals, consolidations, etc. can be
directed to our SupportNet Sales staff at 1-800-443-1601, voice prompt 6 or
to supportnet.sales@merant.com .

Prior to the new AnswerLab going live and the old AnswerLab being made a
read-only resource, we did put out a notice on the page path to the
AnswerLab noting the changes that were coming.  Could we have had the notice
available for a longer time frame, perhaps.  However, since this change was
coming about 3 to 4 months after the new SupportNet site, with it's
requirement for having a SupportNet login registration to get to most of the
other services, we figured (rightly or wrongly) that most of the users would
already have registered for their SupportNet login and would be able to
access the new AnswerLab immediately as a result of that.  For those who
used previously saved bookmarks to the old AnswerLab, they would run into
the read-only message if they didn't understand what had happened.  Going
into the AnswerLab via the SupportNet page link would eliminate the problem,
obviously.

Over the past few months, we have made the SupportNet Informer the main
notification mechanism for our SupportNet services.  This is a successor to
the older Web Informer and now makes use of both the web newsletter format
along with E-Mail notification of both new issues of the SupportNet Informer
and, separately, for notifications of new releases, important patches, etc.
The notification of new upgrades and such are made here and in the last
issue, there was the notification regarding the availability of the new Net
Express v3.1 release.  I can't say if this was the official notification
that Tony <Hill> mentioned in his previous note, or not, but it was a
notification.
That notification included the E-Mail address to use to request your v3.1
upgrade.  You can, also, request an upgrade for any Micro Focus branded
product by using the form provided (under the Product Update link on the
SupportNet page) at
http://support.merant.com/websupport/upgstore/mfcdupgrade.asp .  This form
prefills in your company and personal information from your SupportNet
registration profile, thereby reducing the information that you have to fill
in to complete an upgrade request.

In relation to the writer's item regarding his Net Express v3.0 serial
number not working, many of the early Net Express serial numbers had leading
zeros.  In my experience, those serial numbers are only accepted when the
leading zeros are left off - ie only the significant digits are entered.  An
additional problem is that, since the maintenance on his product had lapsed,
his SupportNet registration would not have been automatically approved.
SupportNet registrations are automatically approved when the company that
you register under and the product serial number that you enter match in our
database (ie. the serial number that you enter matches in our records for
the company that you enter).  Any exception to this puts your registration
into a work queue to have one of our people manually look at the
registration and try to resolve the issues (which may be a mis-keyed serial
number, a serial number for a product that was replaced by a later product
update, a change in company name, etc.).  In those cases where the
maintenance on the input serial number has lapsed, the registration is
referred to the SupportNet Sales group so they can contact the customer and
resolve the maintenance contract issue.

Lastly, we will be constantly striving to improve our electronic services
that we make available via our SupportNet Online web site.  Some will
require changes from previous procedures.  We will try to make notifications
of these changes and additions where and when we can.  However, since not
everyone follows the same path to all of the individual services, we cannot
guarantee that every possible user will see the notifications we do make
available. "
```

## Thread: IBM Research Events in Toronto! — 1 msgs

### IBM Research Events in Toronto!

- **From:** Wayne Ho <who@REMOVEca.ibm.com>
- **Date:** 2000-08-31T17:10:14-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.java.programmer,comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol,comp.lang.fortran
- **Message-ID:** `<39AEC9B6.EA582B66@REMOVEca.ibm.com>`

```
IBM Performance Tuning Tools: Research in Toronto

IBM will be conducting special research events this September, 2000 in
Toronto. We are interested in speaking with individuals (preferable in
the Toronto area) with experience Performance Tuning/Monitoring software
applications  (e.g., using Java, C++, etc.).  Performance
Tuning/Monitoring includes looking for memory leaks, hangs and crashes,
or improving the performance of a software application.

YOUR INPUT COUNTS!

This would give us an opportunity to help ensure that IBM's products
will meet the needs of real customers, and it would give individuals an
opportunity to have input on new products from IBM. The research
directly involves developers of IBM's Application Development tools and
participants' input can definitely impact the software being developed
at the IBM Toronto Lab. This research event may involve visiting the IBM
Toronto Lab and participating in a discussion or helping us evaluate IBM
products. Participants will receive a cash honorarium at the conclusion
of the session for their time.

HOW TO CONTACT IBM

If you are interested, please contact Wayne Ho by e-mail or phone with
the following information (if you feel uncomfortable sending any of this
information, please feel free to simply call me, and I can answer your
questions):

First Name:
Last Name:
Phone number:
E-mail address:
Java Experience (months, years):
C++ Experience (months, years):
OO Experience (months, years):
Experience with Performance Tools (e.g., JProbe, OptimizeIt!,
 Rational Quantify/Coverage, etc.).  Please list the tools you have
used:
Company/Employer:
Job Title (e.g., developer, architect):

 Wayne Ho can be reached at:
 E-mail: who@ca.ibm.com
 Phone: (416)448-4461 (please leave voicemail if unavailable)

My apologies for the cross-post.  Feel free to pass this message on to
people that may be interested in these events.
```
